jokes.text: Andy's a wittle scawed
jokes.setup: Michael is confronting Andy about baby talk complaints
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately demonstrates the exact behavior he's criticizing by using baby talk himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Michael's complete lack of self-awareness. The irony is immediate and structurally elegant.

jokes.text: wittle-ittle, footy-wutties, num nums, jammies, make boom-boom, widicuwous, wode iwand
jokes.setup: Michael reading off Andy's baby talk infractions
jokes.punchline: The sheer volume and ridiculousness of baby talk words, especially 'widicuwous' and 'wode iwand'
jokes.explanation: The escalating list builds comedy through accumulation. 'Widicuwous' and 'wode iwand' are particularly funny phonetic spellings.

jokes.text: Well, if I do, andy's sowwy
jokes.setup: Andy claiming he doesn't use baby talk
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses baby talk in his denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency. Andy's complete lack of self-awareness delivered with economy.

jokes.text: a lot of people think your elvis voice is annoying
jokes.setup: Andy deflecting criticism of his baby talk
jokes.punchline: Immediately throws Michael under the bus with a devastating counter-criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's petty revenge while under pressure.

jokes.text: Tank you, Mr. Elwis
jokes.setup: Michael just complimented Andy's baby voice after criticizing Elvis voice
jokes.punchline: Andy combines both criticized speech patterns in one phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback combining both criticized speech patterns. Maximum comedy from minimal words. Perfect character button to the scene.

jokes.text: The entire office has come down with a pernicious case of the mondays
jokes.setup: Andy doing a medical roleplay bit with Jim
jokes.punchline: Using medical terminology for the common expression 'case of the mondays'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay mixing medical and casual language. Fits Andy's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Bear stearns. Lehman brothers. Aig. My summer at enron.
jokes.setup: Andy listing awesome places he's worked that had employee of the month
jokes.punchline: All the companies are famous financial disasters/scandals
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy revealing Andy's obliviousness to his terrible track record. Perfect character integration with real-world references.

jokes.text: There's about 30 news alerts for 'nip slip'
jokes.setup: Erin innocently going through Michael's email
jokes.punchline: Revealing Michael's inappropriate email habits
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor from Michael's inappropriate behavior. Predictable but fits character.

jokes.text: Must be hackers
jokes.setup: Michael caught with inappropriate email alerts
jokes.punchline: Blaming hackers for his own search habits
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's reflexive deflection. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Did you murder someone? - Worse than that. - Oh, my god.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what could be worse than murder
jokes.punchline: Michael claiming his thing is worse than murder
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that sets up the episode's central crisis. Michael's dramatic overstatement is in character.

jokes.text: Lurk much?
jokes.setup: Camera catches Jim's conversation with Michael
jokes.punchline: Jim addressing the documentary camera directly
jokes.explanation: Meta humor acknowledging the documentary format. Brief but effective character moment for Jim.

jokes.text: But I work hard, I love this company, and for those reasons, I think I would make a good employee of the month
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he can't win employee of the month
jokes.punchline: Immediately making his case for why he should win it
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael contradiction showing his inability to follow his own rules. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: In approximately six hours, jim will get a phone call from david wallace. He will be fired.
jokes.setup: Dwight's talking head about his scheme
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confident prediction of Jim's doom
jokes.explanation: Sets up Dwight's scheme with characteristic confidence. Good setup for later payoff.

jokes.text: Has it really been ten years?
jokes.setup: Seeing the Scott's Tots newspaper article
jokes.punchline: Michael's delayed realization of the time passage
jokes.explanation: Michael's obliviousness to time and consequences beginning to hit

jokes.text: To change lives. - No, michael, why would you promise that?
jokes.setup: Erin asking why Michael promised college tuition
jokes.punchline: Michael's noble answer completely missing the practical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment showing his idealism conflicting with reality. Good character writing.

jokes.text: I've made some empty promises in my life, but hands down, that was the most generous.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on his promise in talking head
jokes.punchline: Proudly calling his empty promise 'the most generous'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Michael's twisted logic. The contradiction is perfectly constructed and quotable.

jokes.text: I bet if you tried, you could grow the best beard of anyone in this office
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to be nice to Jim as part of his scheme
jokes.punchline: The awkward, forced compliment about beard-growing ability
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from Dwight's unnatural attempt at friendliness. The specificity of the compliment makes it funny.

jokes.text: I didn't mean any of those things I just said
jokes.setup: Dwight just gave Jim awkward compliments
jokes.punchline: Immediately confessing to the camera that he was lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment showing his inability to maintain deception. Good use of talking head format.

jokes.text: In an ideal world, I would have all ten fingers on my left hand so my right hand could just be a fist for punching
jokes.setup: Discussion about ideal world scenarios
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarrely violent ideal world involving hand reconfiguration
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist humor perfectly tailored to Dwight's character. Highly original, unexpected, and instantly quotable. Peak Dwight logic.

jokes.text: You didn't even hear it.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming the saxophone performance would blow your mind
jokes.punchline: Erin pointing out Michael wasn't there
jokes.explanation: Simple reality check on Michael's false enthusiasm

jokes.text: Hey, Mr. Scott what you gonna do? Make our dreams come true?
jokes.setup: Students performing a song for Michael
jokes.punchline: The innocent song about dreams coming true when Michael knows he's about to crush them
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy with devastating dramatic irony. The song became iconic for how uncomfortable it makes viewers. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: So jim thought it would be ideal if we all pitched in a twenty
jokes.setup: Andy implementing Dwight's fake scheme
jokes.punchline: Claiming Jim wants money from everyone for employee of the month
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dwight's scheme to frame Jim. Works as part of larger plot structure.

jokes.text: You are a dream maker
jokes.setup: Principal praising Michael before the revelation
jokes.punchline: Calling him a 'dream maker' when he's about to destroy dreams
jokes.explanation: Devastating dramatic irony building the cringe factor before Michael's confession. Well-timed for maximum discomfort.

jokes.text: I just want to thank you for giving me the opportunity to go to college, educate myself, and become the next president obama
jokes.setup: Student giving heartfelt thank you speech
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning becoming 'the next president obama' as a career goal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing youthful ambition. Adds to the cringe by showing how much hope Michael is about to crush.

jokes.text: I did not. That was not. How come not?
jokes.setup: Jim winning employee of the month in rigged contest
jokes.punchline: Jim's panicked, broken speech pattern as he realizes the setup
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing Jim's genuine confusion and panic. The broken speech pattern sells the moment.

jokes.text: yeah, but in your perfect world, only you get money
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the money prize comment
jokes.punchline: Dwight's accusation that Jim's perfect world is selfish
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Dwight's scheme. Shows his ability to twist Jim's words against him.

jokes.text: But you know what's invaluable is intuition. You know what that is? That is the ability to know when something is about to happen.
jokes.setup: Michael promising tuition but pivoting to talking about intuition
jokes.punchline: Michael's pseudo-profound explanation of intuition as stalling tactic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's desperate stalling. The wordplay between 'tuition' and 'intuition' is clever.

jokes.text: Well, zion, I am not going to be paying for your tuition
jokes.setup: Michael finally forced to deliver the bad news
jokes.punchline: Delivering it to the wrong kid first, then having to generalize
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment as Michael finally admits the truth. The specificity of addressing the wrong kid first makes it worse.

jokes.text: Some people have evil dreams. Some people have selfish dreams or wet dreams. My dream was in the right place.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to defend his intentions
jokes.punchline: Including 'wet dreams' in his list of dream types to children
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate comment to children. The casual inclusion of 'wet dreams' is perfectly inappropriate for the situation.

jokes.text: But you don't have to go to class to be in class
jokes.setup: Michael trying to salvage the situation
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical pseudo-wisdom about online learning
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate attempt to sound wise while offering inadequate alternatives. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Which is rendered useless without batteries. And I have one for each of you.
jokes.setup: Michael talking about laptops for online courses
jokes.punchline: Offering laptop batteries instead of laptops or tuition
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdity - the most useless possible 'gift' presented as generous. Peak Michael Scott.

jokes.text: And I have one for each of you
jokes.setup: Michael building up to his laptop battery 'gift'
jokes.punchline: The pathetic inadequacy of batteries as college tuition replacement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy showing the absurd inadequacy of Michael's 'solution'. The gap between promise and delivery is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: They're lithium
jokes.setup: Students rejecting the laptop batteries
jokes.punchline: Michael's proud clarification of battery type as if that helps
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to the battery scene. Michael's pride in the battery quality makes the inadequacy even funnier. Peak cringe delivered with economy.

jokes.text: Guys, I'm starting to think Pam's not even pregnant.
jokes.setup: The suspicious employee of the month results
jokes.punchline: Angela's conspiracy theory about Pam's pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela paranoia taking the conspiracy to absurd lengths

jokes.text: Son of a bitch! Pam halpert.
jokes.setup: Revealing the second employee of the month winner
jokes.punchline: It's Jim's wife, making the scheme even more obvious
jokes.explanation: Good escalation making Dwight's scheme more transparent. Dwight's reaction sells the moment.

jokes.text: Oh, really, from what, two to four? - yep.
jokes.setup: Kevin defending his doubled sales
jokes.punchline: Admitting his 'doubled' sales went from two to four
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment showing his low performance with deadpan pride. The 'yep' response is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: This is kevin malone. Is david there?
jokes.setup: Kevin calling David Wallace's office
jokes.punchline: Kevin directly calling the CEO to complain about Jim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Dwight's scheme is working. Kevin's directness in calling the CEO is in character.

jokes.text: Tell him I'm mad at Jim, 'cause he's asking us to give money to Pam.
jokes.setup: Kevin calling David Wallace
jokes.punchline: Kevin's simplified understanding of the situation
jokes.explanation: Kevin's simple interpretation of the complex employee of the month scandal

jokes.text: It's about $1,000. Really. That's... That's over $200 a year.
jokes.setup: Erin saying textbooks cost $1,000 per year
jokes.punchline: Michael's terrible math making it seem reasonable
jokes.explanation: Michael's bad math attempting to make his obligation seem smaller

jokes.text: Jim, I bumped you up because I thought you could make my life easier. If you can't do that, we're gonna have a separate discussion.
jokes.setup: David Wallace calling about the employee of the month situation
jokes.punchline: Threatening Jim's job over the manufactured crisis
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Dwight's scheme. Shows the real consequences of the office drama escalating to corporate.

jokes.text: 15 lives. I destroyed 15 young lives today.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on the Scott's Tots disaster
jokes.punchline: Michael's dramatic self-assessment of the damage
jokes.explanation: Michael's guilt manifesting as dramatic overstatement

jokes.text: The principal told me that 90% of Scott's Tots are on track to graduate and that's 35% higher than the rest of the school.
jokes.setup: Michael feeling terrible about destroying lives
jokes.punchline: The kids actually performed better because of his promise
jokes.explanation: Beautiful irony that reframes Michael's 'failure' - his lie actually helped motivate the kids

jokes.text: The principal told me that 90% of scott's tots are on track to graduate and that's 35% higher than the rest of the school
jokes.setup: Michael feeling terrible about destroying 15 lives
jokes.punchline: Statistics showing Michael actually helped by motivating the students
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twist showing unintended positive consequences. Adds depth to the cringe by suggesting Michael actually helped despite failing to deliver.

jokes.text: You know, when I hired kevin, he was actually applying for a job in the warehouse
jokes.setup: Michael talking about having feelings about employees
jokes.punchline: Revealing Kevin was hired for the wrong job entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation explaining so much about Kevin's incompetence. Perfect callback that recontextualizes his entire character. Highly quotable and earned.

jokes.text: I want to take jim halpert down. I want in.
jokes.setup: Ryan discovering Dwight's scheme
jokes.punchline: Ryan wanting to join the plot against Jim
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Ryan joining schemes. Sets up future plot development but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: I found a copy of it in the copier tray.
jokes.setup: Ryan knowing about Dwight's diabolical plan
jokes.punchline: Dwight left his secret plan in the copier
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight incompetence - leaving evidence of his schemes lying around
